visualization.title: Average exam score by academic year

resultRaw.sql:
WITH per_row AS (
  SELECT
    CASE
      WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM grade_date) >= 8 THEN (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM grade_date))::int
      ELSE (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM grade_date))::int - 1
    END AS academic_year_start,
    CASE
      WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM grade_date) >= 8
      THEN CONCAT((EXTRACT(YEAR FROM grade_date))::int, '-', ((EXTRACT(YEAR FROM grade_date))::int + 1))
      ELSE CONCAT(((EXTRACT(YEAR FROM grade_date))::int - 1), '-', (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM grade_date))::int)
    END AS academic_year,
    exam_score
  FROM public.grades g
  WHERE g.exam_score IS NOT NULL
    AND g.grade_date IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT
  academic_year AS academic_year,
  AVG(exam_score) AS avg_exam_score
FROM per_row
GROUP BY academic_year_start, academic_year
ORDER BY academic_year_start ASC;

issues: The visualization encodes the exactly aliased fields academic_year and avg_exam_score which exist in metadata.columns — mapping is correct.

academic_year is derived from a date and represents a temporal sequence; using an ordinal x type is acceptable, but using a temporal or numeric (academic_year_start) axis would better reflect a time series and ensure proper ordering/scale.
The y-axis scale has "zero": false which can exaggerate perceptual changes in average scores; consider including zero for a more interpretable baseline when showing averages.
metadata.rowCount is 3 but the sample contains only 2 rows — metadata inconsistency (warning about result metadata, not the visualization itself).

If you want the line to reflect true chronological spacing (e.g., gaps if years missing) or enable time-based axis features, replace the academic_year string with a numeric/temporal field (academic_year_start) in the encoding.

WITH per_row AS (
  SELECT
    CASE
      WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM grade_date) >= 8 THEN (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM grade_date))::int
      ELSE (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM grade_date))::int - 1
    END AS academic_year_start,
    CASE
      WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM grade_date) >= 8
      THEN CONCAT((EXTRACT(YEAR FROM grade_date))::int, '-', ((EXTRACT(YEAR FROM grade_date))::int + 1))
      ELSE CONCAT(((EXTRACT(YEAR FROM grade_date))::int - 1), '-', (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM grade_date))::int)
    END AS academic_year,
    exam_score
  FROM public.grades g
  WHERE g.exam_score IS NOT NULL
    AND g.grade_date IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT
  academic_year AS academic_year,
  AVG(exam_score) AS avg_exam_score
FROM per_row
GROUP BY academic_year_start, academic_year
ORDER BY academic_year_start ASC;
